name: Golden Plains Wind Farm East
current_developer: Golden Plains WF1 Pty Ltd as trustee for the Golden Plains WF1 Unit Trust
notable: Stage 1 of Australias largest wind farm. 122x Vestas V162-6.2MW. Snowy Hydro 40% + Equinix 20% PPAs. A$2B. TagEnergy/IKEA.

timeline.title: East section construction began
timeline.detail: Turbine installation commenced for eastern section

timeline.title: COD estimate updated
timeline.detail: COD changed from 2025-12 to 2025-12-31 (source: AEMO Generation Information)

eis_specs.document_title: Golden Plains Wind Farm Stage 1B (East) — EES Amendment (VIC DEECA)
eis_specs.hub_height_note: Same hub height as Stage 1A (West) — flat Western Victoria plateau
eis_specs.wind_speed_period: Mean annual wind speed at hub height; Golden Plains plateau, Western Victoria — same met mast array as Stage 1A
eis_specs.energy_yield_method: WAsP modelling with ERA5 long-term correction; P50 net yield — consistent methodology with Stage 1A
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Golden Plains 220 kV substation (new, shared with Stage 1A), Western Victoria
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~25 km of 220 kV connection shared with Stage 1A. Both stages connect to the same new on-site substation.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: Shared 220 kV connection with Stage 1A. New substation built to accommodate the full 1,488 MW Golden Plains complex. Part of the Western Victoria Transmission Network Project corridor.
eis_specs.notes: 756 MW (122 x 6.2 MW Vestas V162-6.2 EnVentus), Western Victoria (Golden Plains Shire). Developer: Golden Plains Wind Farm (Vena Energy / Vestas). Stage 1B EES Amendment approved 2021, commissioning 2024. Combined Golden Plains complex (Stage 1A + 1B) totals ~1,488 MW with 240 turbines. Western Victoria Wind Farm Zone — among Australia's best onshore wind resource.
